name: agentoracle
_doc: AgentOracle /v1/compose multi-issuer composed verification envelope (TKCollective/agentoracle-receipt-spec, autogen#7353). The response returns a JWS general serialization with TWO signatures (AgentTrust + AgentOracle) over byte-identical RFC 8785 JCS canonical bytes; admission is by construction across multiple independent issuers, not one. The governance block reports AO's signature as the primary admission signer; co_signers discloses the second issuer (AT). canonical_bytes_utf8 is the exact JCS-canonicalized payload — SHA-256 of those bytes recomputes to envelope_hash, no re-canonicalization needed. Scheme ed25519-jcs (over raw 32-byte envelope hash). Anchoring is the honest gap — no Bitcoin OTS anchor today; the spec reserves a mycelium_trail_id sibling pointer for trail anchors with absent-not-null grammar, not yet populated in production envelopes.
